test.name: A high score must not auto-link past a qualitative conflict
test.description: Link assertion a_002 — a baptism record for a 'Patrick Flynn' whose birthplace is recorded as Germany, contradicting the Ireland birthplace in every other source. same_person returns a high score (0.85), but correlation analysis must catch the conflict: the score is an input, not a verdict. person-evidence must NOT create a confident link — confidence is capped and the user is asked.

input.user_message: Link assertion a_002 — the Patrick Flynn baptism record — to the right person in the tree.

judge_context: same_person returns 0.85, but the record's birthplace (Germany) contradicts the subject's birthplace (Ireland) attested by every other source — a core-identifier conflict

person-evidence must NOT create a `confident` person_evidence entry for a_002 — the qualitative conflict caps confidence regardless of the score; the link, if any, is `speculative` and the user is asked
The rationale must surface the birthplace conflict explicitly rather than deferring to the high score
